subject: English
chapter: Poems – How to Tell Wild Animals, The Ball Poem

activities_projects.title: Detailed Animal Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Explore characteristics and behavior of animals mentioned in the poem.
steps: Read 'How to Tell Wild Animals' carefully
List all animals and note their unique features or behaviors
Write a paragraph analyzing how these traits reflect the poet’s humor or observation skills
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand descriptive and observational techniques in poetry.
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness, relevance, and clarity of analysis

activities_projects.title: Humor Interpretation
activities_projects.objective: Understand how humor is used in poetry.
steps: Identify lines that are humorous or ironic
Explain why they are funny and how the poet creates humor
Share explanations in pairs or groups
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students interpret and appreciate humorous literary devices.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification and explanation of humor

activities_projects.title: Analyzing Emotions in 'The Ball Poem'
activities_projects.objective: Explore the theme of loss and maturity.
steps: Read 'The Ball Poem' carefully
Identify the boy’s emotions at different stages of losing the ball
Write a paragraph connecting these feelings to real-life experiences of loss
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop emotional understanding and relate literary themes to life.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and relevance to personal reflection

activities_projects.title: Rhyme and Sound Patterns
activities_projects.objective: Examine rhyme, rhythm, and sound in the poems.
steps: List rhyming words and note the rhyme scheme
Explain how rhyme and rhythm enhance meaning or humor
Compare differences in sound patterns between the two poems
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect poetic sound techniques to meaning and tone.

activities_projects.title: Imagery and Visualization
activities_projects.objective: Understand and interpret imagery in both poems.
steps: Select one stanza from either poem
Draw a scene visualizing the description and emotions
Write 2–3 lines explaining your illustration
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students interpret imagery and connect visual creativity with literary comprehension.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, accuracy, and clarity of explanation

activities_projects.title: Role-Play – Expressing Emotions
steps: Act out the boy losing the ball or a humorous animal action
Use voice and gestures to express emotions
Discuss how role-playing improves understanding of the poem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve empathy, expression, and comprehension of themes.
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation, clarity, and expression of emotions

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Expansion
activities_projects.objective: Learn and apply new words from both poems.
steps: Identify 6–8 new words from each poem
Write meanings, synonyms, and use them in sentences
Discuss the effect of word choice on tone and meaning
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students expand vocabulary and understand the effect of word choice in poetry.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct meanings, sentence usage, and relevance

activities_projects.title: Tone Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Compare tone between humorous and serious poems.
steps: Identify tone of each poem (humorous, serious, reflective)
Provide textual evidence for your opinion
Write a short note comparing how tone changes reader perception
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop analytical skills and understand how tone affects interpretation.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and clarity in tone analysis

activities_projects.title: Comprehension Questions
activities_projects.objective: Test understanding of themes and details.
steps: Answer questions such as 'Why does the poet use humor?' or 'How does the boy learn from losing the ball?'
Write full answers with references to the text
Discuss responses in pairs or groups
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve comprehension and textual analysis skills.

activities_projects.title: Poem Retelling with Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Summarize and interpret poems in own words.
steps: Retell each poem in 5–6 sentences
Include main idea, tone, and important emotions
Share with a classmate or teacher
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students strengthen summarization, comprehension, and interpretive skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity, accuracy, and inclusion of key points
